name: Connecticut (United States)

colors.symbolism: the colour of the field, evoking the flags of the colonial era

description: An azure-blue field bearing in the centre a white rococo shield charged with three grapevines, each with three bunches of purple grapes and green leaves; below the shield is a white scroll with the Latin motto 'QUI TRANSTULIT SUSTINET' in dark blue letters.
history: The grapevines and motto have been associated with Connecticut since the 17th century, appearing on the colonial seal of Saybrook (1639) and later the colony as a whole. The arms in their modern form were settled by the General Assembly in 1882, and the flag itself — placing the arms on a blue field — was adopted by an Act of the General Assembly on 9 June 1897.

features.role: three grapevines on the shield, each bearing fruit
features.symbolism: the three early Connecticut colonies (Hartford, New Haven, and Saybrook) transplanted from Europe

features.role: Latin motto 'QUI TRANSTULIT SUSTINET' on a white scroll beneath the shield
features.symbolism: 'He who transplanted still sustains' — taken from Psalm 80, referring to God's protection of those transplanted from England
